metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the generated email includes all required key facts accurately and naturally.

rubric.description: The email ignores the required facts or contradicts them.
Only a tiny fragment of one required fact appears.
Some facts are present, but multiple important facts are missing or distorted.
Most facts are present, but one important fact is missing, weakened, or ambiguous.
Almost all required facts are present, with only a minor omission or awkward compression.
Every required fact is included accurately and naturally, with no unsupported factual claims.

metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the generated email matches the requested tone in context.

rubric.description: The tone is absent, opposite of the requested tone, or unusable for the context.
The tone is very far from the requested tone.
The tone only loosely resembles the requested tone and is noticeably mismatched.
The tone is partly aligned but generic, uneven, or inconsistent.
The tone is mostly aligned with only small mismatches.
The tone closely and consistently matches the requested tone throughout.

metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the output works as a professional email.

rubric.description: The output is not usable as an email.
It barely resembles a professional email or collapses greeting, body, closing, and signature into a flat single paragraph.
Some email elements are present, but structure is incomplete, hard to scan, or mostly unformatted.
Basic email content is present, but paragraphing, greeting, body, or sign-off formatting is noticeably weak.
The email is clear and professional with only minor structure or formatting rough edges.
The subject and body are polished, clear, concise, and formatted with distinct greeting, body paragraphing, and sign-off.

metrics.reason: The email includes all required facts: it references the May 17 meeting at 11:00 A.M., names Kenneth Lay, Marty Sunde, and Bob Williams, and clearly asks where the attendees should wait or go when they arrive. It is polite, professional, and aligned with the stated intent.
The email is concise, polite, and professional throughout. It directly and respectfully asks for the exact waiting instructions for the May 17 meeting, matching the requested tone very closely.
The email has a clear subject line, a professional greeting, a concise body in a separate paragraph, and a proper sign-off with signature. It is well structured and easy to scan.

All required facts are included accurately: the email proposes a project review meeting next week, lists the three available slots (Tuesday 2–3 PM, Wednesday 10–11 AM, Thursday 1–2 PM), asks recipients to reply with their availability for those time slots, and says the meeting should be finalized soon.

The email is consistently professional, concise, and collaborative. It clearly proposes next-week meeting options, asks recipients to share availability, and politely emphasizes the need to finalize soon without sounding abrupt.

The email is well-structured and professional: it includes a clear subject, a greeting, a concise body with distinct paragraphing, a direct request for availability on specific time slots, and a proper sign-off with name and title. It is polished and easy to scan.

The email includes all required facts accurately: it identifies invoice 48217, states it was due on April 30, 2026, notes the outstanding amount is $3,450, and asks for an update on the expected payment date. The tone and sender/recipient details are also consistent with the scenario.

The email is firm, polite, and professional throughout: it states the overdue invoice and amount clearly, asks directly for the expected payment date, and closes with courteous language such as "please," "thank you," and "Best regards."

The email has a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, well-separated body paragraphs, and a proper sign-off with signature. It is polished, concise, and professional, matching the rubric for a top score.

The email includes all required facts: it says the interview was originally set for Tuesday at 2:00 PM, explains the interviewer has a conflict, offers Wednesday at 9:00 AM or Thursday at 3:00 PM, identifies the Software Engineer position at BrightPath Software Solutions, notes the interview will last about 90 minutes, and asks Avery to confirm which time works best. No required facts are missing or contradicted.

The email is professional and concise, includes an apology for the interviewer conflict, and politely asks Avery to choose between the proposed reschedule options. It matches the requested courteous, apologetic tone throughout.

The email has a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, a well-structured body, and a professional sign-off with signature. It is concise, polite, and easy to scan, with no notable formatting issues.

The email includes all required facts accurately: it reminds recipients that early-bird registration closes on September 10, 2025; gives the conference dates as October 22 to 24, 2025; states the early-bird fee is $300 and the regular fee is $400; and explicitly encourages people to register soon to get the lower rate. It also includes the North Shore Innovation Summit affiliation.

The email is consistently informative, upbeat, and professional: it gives the deadline, dates, and pricing clearly, uses a friendly reminder tone, and encourages prompt registration without becoming overly casual or salesy.

The email includes a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, two well-separated body paragraphs, and a professional sign-off with signature. The structure is polished and easy to scan, with no notable formatting issues.

The email includes all required facts accurately: it says the design review is expected to finish by Friday, 19 June, 2026; it attributes the delay to missing stakeholder feedback; and it states there is no change needed to the final launch date yet.

The email is transparent about the delay and its cause, calm in wording, and reassuring by stating the final launch date is unchanged and that progress will be monitored. The tone is consistent throughout with no notable mismatch.
The email has a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, well-separated body paragraphs, and a proper sign-off. The structure is polished and easy to scan, matching the highest rubric level.

All required facts are included accurately: the meeting covered Q3 roadmap priorities, the attached notes summarize the key decisions and action items, and the reader is asked to send corrections by end of day Thursday. The sender and affiliation also match the scenario.

The email is clear, organized, and professional throughout. It states the meeting topic, references the attached notes, and politely requests corrections by the deadline, closely matching the requested tone.

The email has a clear subject, a professional greeting, a well-structured body that states the meeting topic and request, and a proper sign-off with signature. It is polished, concise, and easy to scan.

The email includes all required facts accurately: it invites volunteers to a community cleanup on Saturday from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM, asks them to bring gloves and closed-toe shoes, and states that lunch and water will be provided. It also matches the friendly, appreciative tone and identifies the Community Outreach Organizer and Riverside Neighborhood Association.

The email is consistently friendly, encouraging, and appreciative throughout: it warmly invites volunteers, emphasizes the positive impact of their help, and thanks them sincerely for their time and willingness to help.

The email includes a clear subject, a distinct greeting, two short body paragraphs, and a proper sign-off with signature. It is polished, easy to scan, and professionally formatted for the volunteer invitation.

The email includes all required facts accurately: visitors must check in at the front desk, a government-issued photo ID is required for entry, and the new policy starts on Monday. The tone is also formal and informative.

The email is consistently formal and informative, with polite language such as 'Please be advised' and 'Thank you for your attention to this update.' It clearly communicates the policy change, the front desk check-in requirement, the photo ID requirement, and the Monday start date in a professional tone.

The email includes a clear subject line, a proper greeting, a concise body paragraph with the key policy changes and effective date, and a professional sign-off. The structure is polished and easy to scan, matching the highest rubric level.

The email includes all required facts accurately: maintenance on Sunday from 1:00 AM to 3:00 AM, the portal may be unavailable during that window, and service is expected to resume immediately after maintenance. The tone is brief, reassuring, and professional.

The email is brief, professional, and reassuring throughout. It clearly states the maintenance window, notes that the portal may be unavailable, and reassures users that service should resume immediately after maintenance, which closely matches the requested tone.

The email has a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, a well-formatted body in a separate paragraph, and a proper sign-off with signature placeholder. It is concise, professional, and well structured, matching the rubric for a polished email.
